title: Dedication of a marble basin
support: A large stone krater, or vase, with simple pedestal foot, rim, and two handles. Judged by Ferrua to be a compact local limestone, rather than a marble. The text is heavily worn and not entirely legible

layout.p: Three lines of text, on one side of the basin, with line 1 on the outside of the rim, and lines 2-3 on the belly of the krater below the rim.

handNote.lettering: Large, quite finely cut Greek letters: Alpha with straight bar, Epsilon with shorter middle bar, theta and omicron full size, xsi of the form of a Z with split and curled diagonal, standard four-bar sigma, omega of classical form, partly open.

provenanceFound._: First seen and described by Ottavio Gaetani (1566-1620), who observed it in the chapel of Castello Maniace (Ortygia), but who recorded that it had previously been transported there by the spanish commander, from the church of St. John (i.e. the catacombs of S. Giovanni); it was then moved to the Duomo in 1644 and after the earthquake of 1693 replaced the old baptismal font.
